== Notes for [[Item:Q103488|Q103488]] ==

Humanist, writer, translator of Plutarco and friend of Luis Vives of the first half of the XVI century. Translated Luis Vives most famous pedagogical work "Introductio Sapientiae", published in Antwerp in 1551.

https://books.google.se/books?id=XEsf9WY-qykC&pg=PA101&dq=diego+de+astudillo+luis+vives&hl=en&sa=X&ved=0ahUKEwje3Y_3gpDdAhUBsywKHd9yBr4Q6AEIRDAE#v=onepage&q=diego%20de%20astudillo%20luis%20vives&f=false

https://books.google.se/books?id=P9-MBA0gDMoC&pg=PA73&lpg=PA73&dq=diego+de+astudillo&source=bl&ots=scQghgkt8m&sig=oNgyS4L9qa7WvDA4HY49hERQjw4&hl=en&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwi-1pnHgJDdAhVDFiwKHeBcDm4Q6AEwDHoECAIQAQ#v=onepage&q=diego%20de%20astudillo&f=false

REFERENCE_NUMBERS:
geni:6000000081067754170

[[Category:GEDCOM Notes Pages]][[Category:Pages]]
